nodes.context: Mexican drug cartel formed in late 1970s, controlled much of Mexico's drug trafficking in 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: Multiple modern present day drug cartels (or their remnants) such as the Tijuana , Juárez and Sinaloa cartels originally started out as branches or "plazas" of the Guadalajara Cartel before its eventual disintegration.
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel started as a branch of Guadalajara Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Rafael Caro Quintero and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo ("Don Neto"), who previously worked in the Avilés criminal organization , took control of the trafficking routes after Avilés was killed in a shootout with MFJP police officers.
edges.context: Members of Guadalajara Cartel previously worked in Avilés organization and took over its routes.
